GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Meet 'My Way', the new elegant scent from Giorgio Armani", "While it may be true that Covid-19 has upended our usual routines - with many of us working remotely and adapting to a new normal - the ritual of spritzing a fragrance onto the body before starting one's day is a calming constant in turbulent times. Perfumes can affect our mood, rekindle our senses, help evoke our personality and, energetically, reset the kind of spirit or vibe we are communicating through scent. This is something Milanese luxury house Giorgio Armani knows well, and has carefully distilled in their newest offering from Armani Beauty, a floral fragrance called My Way. Designed to evoke a feminine inner confidence, underlined by a natural curiosity and desire for connection in one's personal journey, My Way was developed with sustainability in mind. Its multilayered notes are built out from climate-conscious ingredients sourced from all over the world. Think a hint of bergamot sourced from Italy's Calabria, as it fuses gently with orange blossom derived from Egypt. Then, a light and feminine mix of tuberose and jasmine gives the fragrance delicate nuance at its base. These meet the scent of cedar wood sourced from Virginia, white musks, and vanilla sourced from Madagascar that rounds out the scent's worldly-yet-elegant profile. It is at once reminiscent of travels past, able to conjure memories and write new ones, and forward-looking to a future where we can wander freely and explore the world, and ourselves, again. Not only future-facing in its curated palette of scents, sourced from all over the world, My Way also signifies Giorgio Armani's continued sustainability efforts and commitment to fight climate change. The fragrance's bottle is easily refillable, designed with recycled and recyclable materials, and so can be proudly displayed in one's bathroom cabinet or beauty shelf for years to come. It is carbon neutral and in step with Armani Beauty's wider 5-year action plan to achieve carbon neutrality across all of its products by 2025 and reduce its carbon footprint by 25%. As Giorgio Armani reflected in a press release: "I believe that environmental issues should be close to everyone's heart, now more than ever. There is no way we can ignore the fact that the future of the younger generations depends on our choices." That's why the luxury house has aligned with actress Adria Arjona, who fronts the campaign, and whose own ethos aligns with that of My Way - which sees elegance, individuality and a sustainability-first mindset coalesce in a philosophy defined by 'I am what I live'. Speaking to Arjona's appointment as the face of My Way, and a new face of Armani Beauty, Armani said: "Adria Arjona is a traveler, a young woman of today; intelligent, curious, free of prejudice, independent, and capable of treasuring every life experience. She is a citizen of the world, who thanks to her authentic experiences and encounters can truly say: I am what I live." Now, with a spray or two of your own bottle of My Way, you can too. My Way is available to buy here."
